expectations.evidence: ls -la confirms /tmp/peekie-eval-failed-attachments/ contains 36 entries (35 files + manifest.json).

FAIL — directory has 35 attachment files; only 4 actually belong to failing tests. The agent itself called this out as a 'heads-up gotcha' — said peekie's --include failure filters the JSON manifest but xcresulttool writes every attachment to disk regardless. Skill's claim that --include failure 'pushes down to xcresulttool --only-failures' is misleading here.
Markdown table maps each of the 4 valid attachments to its failing test + repetition + content type, plus full ls -la output.
FAIL — 31 non-failure attachments leaked onto disk (PNGs and other text blobs from passing tests).
